title: Monitoring and Reporting Platform
description: This project involved redesigning an internal management and control platform used by employees to manage operational processes, monitor information, and improve communication across different business areas.
challenge: Simplify complex internal processes and redesign legacy workflows to improve operational efficiency, communication, and usability for hundreds of employees.

sections.col1Content: Internal management and operational control platform.
sections.col2Content: UX Research, Information Architecture, UX/UI Design, Design System, Usability Testing

sections.col1Content: Product Owner, Apps Manager, Product Manager, Tech Lead, Developers, Business Reporter, Business Stakeholders
sections.col2Content: Simplify complex internal processes and redesign legacy workflows to improve operational efficiency, communication, and usability for hundreds of employees.

sections.label: Rediseño de Plataforma de Monitoreo y Reporting Bancario
sections.content: Modernización de una plataforma interna utilizada para supervisar, generar y gestionar reportes de transacciones intra-bancarias en tiempo real.

items.title: Design System
items.description: Early adoption of the corporate Design System.

sections.label: 1 · The Challenge
sections.content: How to modernize a critical banking monitoring platform without affecting operational continuity, enabling more efficient real-time event management? The platform had over six years of evolution and was used daily by internal teams to monitor report generation, supervise intra-bank transactions, validate automatic processes, intervene manually on failures, and consult historical information.

sections.label: Domain Understanding · Critical Processes
sections.intro: The platform supports critical processes where an operational error can affect traceability, internal processes, and incident resolution times:

items.title: Modernize
items.description: A critical platform for banking operations.

items.title: Reduce risk
items.description: Minimize operational errors and incident resolution times.

items.title: Design System
items.description: Enable future evolution through a corporate Design System.

items.title: Visibility
items.description: Quickly understand the state of operations.

items.title: Efficiency
items.description: Complete tasks with fewer steps and less navigation.

items.title: Cognitive load
items.description: Reduce mental effort required to monitor and act.

sections.label: Role · Senior Product Designer — End-to-End
sections.intro: Full participation across Discovery, Design, Product Definition, and Delivery & QA. Responsibilities included:
items: Research, interviews, workshops, pain point analysis, validations
Information Architecture, User Flows, Wireframes, UI Design
User Stories and functional requirements documentation
Test cases, functional QA, pre-release validations, incident tracking

sections.label: 2 · Discovery & Research
sections.content: Over 3 years of product evolution, a continuous research process was implemented. Activities included quarterly meetings with business users, semi-annual interviews with end clients, discovery workshops, pain point surveys, and iterative feature validations. Research findings were translated into User Stories, acceptance criteria, functional documentation, and requirement prioritization.

sections.label: 3 · Insights
sections.content: Users need a fast and reliable way to monitor report statuses and act on incidents, as the current platform hinders understanding of operational context and increases response times.

sections.label: Design Strategy
items: Align experience to the new Design System.
Adapt the experience by profile and permissions.

sections.label: 5 · Solution
sections.content: The solution was built on four main pillars: Operational Dashboard, Data Visualization, Status Management, and Safe Manual Intervention.

sections.label: Dashboard as Operational Hub
sections.content: The dashboard was designed as the primary monitoring point, incorporating key metrics, report statuses, alerts, indicators, quick access, and priority actions.

sections.label: Data Visualization
sections.content: Historical and operational information was transformed into charts, indicators, metrics, and trends — enabling anomaly detection, incident identification, and contextual understanding.

sections.label: Optimized Datatables
sections.content: Tables were redesigned to improve legibility, facilitate analysis, reduce cognitive load, and accelerate search. Status management was refined to cover automatic and manual states, immediate feedback, error messages, and context-aware available actions.

sections.label: Progressive Disclosure
sections.content: Dashboard → Carousel + cards → Datatables → Detailed report → Actions. This approach organized accessible information in the dashboard, kept metrics available at all times, and offered different levels of detail based on user need.

sections.label: Role Management
sections.content: Differentiated experiences based on permissions, responsibilities, and operational needs — creator and authorizer roles with adapted views and available actions.

sections.label: Detail Modal
sections.content: Full report visualization with the ability to view detail, edit, and send by email — all without leaving the current workflow.

sections.label: Transition Strategy
sections.content: Migration to the new experience was designed to occur without interrupting daily business operations, through coexistence between previous and new versions, permanent system availability, and progressive migration of features and experiences.

sections.label: 6 · Key Learnings
items: In highly complex operational systems, information visibility is as important as process simplification.
Status visibility is as important as functionality.
Dashboards must function as operational decision-making hubs, not just access points.
Modernizing a legacy product means transforming processes, not just interfaces.

This project allowed me to lead the transformation of a critical banking monitoring platform, balancing operational needs, technical constraints, and user experience while contributing to the Design System's evolution.
